description: Narrative storytelling that traces product journey from source (farm/origin) to consumer, humanizing intermediaries and highlighting craftsmanship at each stage.
structural_recipe: Show the complete journey: origin (farm/harvest) → intermediate handler/processor (name them, humanize them) → final product → consumer moment. Use Arabic narrative connecting each stage with emotional language. Ask engagement question about which stage resonated most. Include emojis representing each phase. Structure as: 'From X to Y... there's a stage that makes the difference' with named character/role carrying the story forward.
why_it_works: Saudi audiences value authenticity, trust, and connection to craftsmanship. Naming specific people (Hussein in example) builds parasocial trust and transparency. The narrative elevates manual labor culturally, aligns with Islamic values of honest work, and creates emotional investment in the product beyond commodity transactionalism.
transplantation_caveats: Requires verified supply chain data—unsubstantiated claims damage credibility in informed Saudi market
Workers featured should be treated with dignity; avoid exploitative framing of labor
Regional sourcing emphasis may limit appeal if product source conflicts with Saudi procurement preferences
cultural_constraints: Must align with Islamic work ethics and dignity of labor
Personalization of supply chain actors preferred over anonymous processes
Transparency messaging should avoid negative implications about alternative suppliers
